message:
Add double-resolution detection and temporal quiescence to oracles

ReplyLinearityOracle:
- Extended pending tuple from (Time, bool) to (Time, bool, bool) adding
  an over_resolved flag that tracks whether a reply was resolved more than
  once (e.g. sent then aborted, or sent twice)
- on_reply_sent/on_reply_aborted now check if already resolved before
  setting the flag, marking over_resolved on the second resolution
- check() now returns a linearity violation for over-resolved replies
  before checking for dropped (un-resolved) ones, catching the dual
  failure mode that was previously silent
- Added test: reply_linearity_fail_on_double_resolution

SupervisorQuiescenceOracle:
- Changed completed_tasks from HashSet<TaskId> to HashMap<TaskId, Time>
  to record when each task completed, keeping the earliest completion
  time on duplicate completions
- on_task_completed now takes a Time parameter and uses entry API with
  and_modify to preserve the minimum completion timestamp
- Quiescence check now filters active children by comparing completion
  time against region close time: a child that completed after the region
  closed is considered still-active, catching a temporal ordering bug
  where a late-completing child could falsely appear quiescent
- Updated existing tests to pass Time::ZERO to on_task_completed
- Added test: supervisor_quiescence_fail_when_child_completes_after_close

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
